title: calpidisca hispida
side_effects: Calpidisca hispida can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, headaches, and dizziness, which can occur due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingestion of the plant's sap or leaves can also lead to stomach pain and vomiting. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may cause respiratory problems, such as shortness of breath and wheezing. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can cause skin irritation, including redness, itching, and blistering.
